task: No, I'll trust your word on that for now. All seems well. Stand by for the moment, thanks.

Ok, this issue identified by the reviewer needs to be verified very carefully and you must push back if you notice any inconsistencies in the rest of the code base or a violation of invariants. Then once confident in the patterns across the project and the scope of the ask, plan a fix that I can review: The migration introduces a production-breaking settings regression by omitting api-version from Extension Management REST calls, and the new Build API fallback logic misclassifies
  legitimate 404s as version-negotiation failures. These issues make the patch unsafe to consider correct.

  Full review comments:

  - [P1] Append an api-version when calling ExtensionManagement REST — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\extension\ui\modules\sdk.ts:227-230
    The new direct settings client builds Extension Management URLs without any api-version query string. Azure DevOps REST endpoints require that parameter, so getValue/setValue will
    start failing in real extension hosts with 400-level responses, which means the settings page can no longer load or persist the saved project/pipeline selection and the dashboard will
    always fall back to auto-discovery. Please include a supported api-version on the URL you build here.
  - [P2] Do not treat every first-call 404 as an unsupported API version — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\extension\ui\artifact-client.ts:273-280
    _fetchWithVersionFallback() currently retries on any uncached 404, but a 404 is also the normal response for missing builds/artifacts/files. On the first request for a stale project/
    build/artifact, settings validation and artifact fetches will now burn through all versions and report a misleading Build API api-version=5.1: 404 negotiation failure instead of the
    actual not-found condition. Only version-specific failures should advance the fallback chain; genuine 404s need to be surfaced to the caller.
user_feedback_text:

task:
We have to plan better, Sorry for interupting. Follow these directions: * **Incorrect success-path caching on 404 for resource endpoints**

  * New logic caches version on any “non-retry” status, including 404 → treats “not found” as a successful version resolution
  * Fix:

    * Only cache version on `response.ok`
    * For resource endpoints:

      * If `status === 404`, **return without caching version**

* **Version resolution contamination across calls**

  * A single 404 on artifact-file will lock in a version that may not actually be valid for future successful calls
  * Fix:

    * Cache version **only after first successful (2xx) response per family**

* **Contract inconsistency between list vs resource fallback**

  * Current logic mixes “version resolution” and “business response handling” in same branch
  * Fix:

    * Separate concerns:

      * Version probing → only based on 400 (+ conditional 404 for list)
      * Response handling → return immediately without affecting version cache unless success

* **Test gap: false positive version resolution**

  * New test asserts 404 returns correctly, but does not assert that version cache remains unset
  * Fix:

    * Add assertion: version cache for `artifact-file` is **undefined after 404 response**

task:
Ok, we are far from complete but on the right track. Now we have two new issues to research thorough and plan carefully 1. [P1] The refactor now pins Azure DevOps bearer tokens for the lifetime of the page, which will break all direct REST paths after token expiry. /E:/projects/ado-git-repo-insights/
     extension/ui/modules/sdk.ts:85, /E:/projects/ado-git-repo-insights/extension/ui/modules/sdk.ts:220, /E:/projects/ado-git-repo-insights/extension/ui/modules/sdk.ts:341, /E:/projects/
     ado-git-repo-insights/extension/ui/artifact-client.ts:71
     The new SDK wrapper caches SDK.getAccessToken() globally, getExtensionDataService() captures that token into a fixed Authorization header once, and ArtifactClient.initialize() stores
     a fixed token as well. That is a behavioral regression from the old SDK/XDM-backed clients: once the delegated token ages out, settings reads/writes, project listing, artifact
     resolution, and raw-download requests will all keep sending a stale bearer and fail with 401s until the user reloads the extension. This needs per-request token refresh or a 401-
     triggered refresh path instead of session-long caching.
  2. [P2] getArtifacts() still misclassifies legitimate 404s as API-version negotiation failures. /E:/projects/ado-git-repo-insights/extension/ui/artifact-client.ts:37, /E:/projects/ado-
     git-repo-insights/extension/ui/artifact-client.ts:273
     The earlier fix narrowed 404 retries to “list families”, but "artifacts" is still in that set. _apis/build/builds/{buildId}/artifacts returns a real 404 when the build ID is stale or
     deleted, so the first uncached request for that build will still burn through all versions and end with Build API api-version=5.1: 404 instead of surfacing the actual missing-build
     condition. That still breaks validation/download flows for stale saved builds with the same misleading error class as before.
user_feedback_text:

sorry interupted, we have an isse that required investigatgion: [P2] getOrganizationProjects() still treats every non-auth failure as API-version negotiation, so real server errors are retried and then misreported as “No compatible API
     version”. /E:/projects/ado-git-repo-insights/extension/ui/settings.ts:211, /E:/projects/ado-git-repo-insights/extension/ui/settings.ts:226
     The artifact client now only advances the fallback chain on version-shaped failures, but the settings page’s separate project-listing logic still does if (!response.ok) { lastError
     = ...; continue; } for everything except 401/403. A real 500/502/503 from _apis/projects will now burn through 7.1 -> 6.0 -> 5.1, then surface a misleading version-negotiation error
     and unnecessarily drop the UI into text-input fallback. That path should only retry on the statuses that actually indicate unsupported API versions, and should surface genuine server
     failures directly.

Do not code. Plan with me and try to avoid the back and forth. You can clearly see how many commits are on this branch. It is mostly due to your lack of foresight. Please plan accordingly. The patch introduces a server-compatibility regression in artifact API fallback and weakens two new policy/parity guards, so it cannot be considered correct as-is. These issues are
  discrete and would likely be fixed once identified.

  Full review comments:

  - [P1] Treat build artifact listing as a list endpoint for fallback — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\extension\ui\artifact-client.ts:40-42
    On Azure DevOps Server instances where /_apis/build/builds/{id}/artifacts returns 404 for an unsupported api-version, this family will never fall back because artifacts is omitted
    from LIST_ENDPOINT_FAMILIES. In that environment getArtifacts() will surface a false “Build … not found” error, which breaks pipeline discovery and every artifact-backed dashboard
    load even though older API versions may work.
  - [P2] Limit version-override marker scanning to branch commits — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\scripts\check-version-unchanged.py:83-84
    Using git log {base_branch}...HEAD also includes commits that exist only on the base branch. If main gains a commit whose subject contains [version-override-acknowledged] after a
    feature branch diverges, this guard will start approving unrelated manual version bumps on that feature branch. The scan needs to look only at commits reachable from HEAD and not from
    the base ref.
  - [P2] Do not report TypeScript statements coverage from line totals — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\scripts\check_coverage_delta.py:80-82
    This guard advertises parity with Codecov's project metrics, but statements is currently hard-wired to the line-rate calculation. In any change where Istanbul statement coverage moves
    independently of line coverage (for example, multiple statements on one covered line), the local guard will silently pa
